title: Secret Handling Policy
body:
This policy governs the handling of secrets across all projects and systems.

All secrets, including but not limited to API keys, authentication tokens, database credentials, encryption keys, and private certificates, must be stored securely and never appear in plain text within any file or repository.

Permitted methods:
- Use environment variables loaded at runtime. Do not commit .env files to version control.
- Use a dedicated secrets vault such as HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, or Azure Key Vault.
- For local development, use encrypted configuration files with strong passphrase protection.

Prohibited practices:
- Hardcoding secrets directly in source code, configuration files, or documentation.
- Sharing secrets via email, chat, or ticketing systems.
- Storing secrets in version control even if the repository is private.

Any hardcoded secret discovered during code review or audits must be reported immediately to the security team, revoked, and replaced with a vault-managed or environment-based alternative. Non-compliance may result in disciplinary action.

For questions or exception requests, contact security@company.internal.
